entities.name: RICHARDS FUNCTION
entities.description: A mathematical function used for modeling crop growth.

entities.name: EXPOLINEAR GROWTH EQUATION
entities.description: A physiologically based mathematical function where crop growth rate is proportional to intercepted radiation and an exponential function of leaf area.

entities.name: CROP GROWTH RATE
entities.description: The rate at which a crop increases in biomass, referred to as CGR.

entities.name: INTERCEPTED RADIATION
entities.description: The amount of light captured by the crop, which can be a limiting factor for growth.

entities.name: LEAF AREA
entities.description: The total area of leaves, which influences the exponential function of growth in the expolinear equation.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A crop used to illustrate the expolinear growth equation.

entities.name: FABA BEANS
entities.description: A crop used to illustrate the expolinear growth equation.

entities.name: OIL PALMS
entities.description: A crop used to illustrate the expolinear growth equation.

entities.name: OPTIMUM GROWTH STRATEGIES
entities.description: Strategies for maximizing crop growth based on the proposed mathematical function.
